paper_title: OmniMoE: An Efficient MoE by Orchestrating Atomic Experts at Scale

problem_statement.moe_bottlenecks: MoEs improve parameter efficiency but suffer from bandwidth bottlenecks due to scattered, fragmented memory accesses
problem_statement.coarse_grained_issues: Coarse-grained MoEs suffer from imprecise activation and low flexibility, leading to computational waste
problem_statement.fine_grained_challenges: Fine-grained MoEs face limited expressivity, routing overhead, and hardware inefficiency

omni_moe_solution.approach: Employs a universally activated shared dense MLP
omni_moe_solution.key_innovation: Uses expert-centric scheduling to reorganize fine-grained expert fetches into contiguous, coalesced memory accesses
omni_moe_solution.benefits: Achieves both high parameter efficiency and hardware-efficient execution

coarse_grained.description: Dominant paradigm in large-scale language models
coarse_grained.implementation: Each expert is a complete dense FFN
coarse_grained.advantages: Hardware-efficient dense matmuls (via Tensor Cores), contiguous VRAM access
coarse_grained.limitations: Imprecise activation, computational waste, rigid size restricts scaling flexibility

fine_grained.description: Utilize millions of lightweight experts (e.g., embeddings)
fine_grained.advantages: Precise routing and fine-grained control over both model capacity and activated parameters
challenges: Limited expressivity: existing designs reduce experts to static parameter vectors, restricting to linear vector aggregation
Routing overhead: scaling to large expert pool increases routing cost and load imbalance
Hardware inefficiency: scattered activations trigger random memory I/O, shifting execution from compute-bound to memory-bound

title: OmniMoE: An Efficient MoE by Orchestrating Atomic Experts at Scale
